title: Where the Pillars Fall

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: He was born of vow and firelight,
A storm wrapped in a quiet frame.
The lion fell beneath his hands,
Yet love would call him by his name.
She came with silks and honeyed words,
A shadow draped in garden skin,
Each touch a thread around his soul,
Each kiss a whisper drawing him in.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: By candle breath and lullaby,
She asked him where his secret lay.
He laughed, then spoke, then laughed again
Until the truth slipped out to stay.
He slept within her braided arms,
Unknowing she had drawn the line.
She called the night, and with one blade,
Unraveled all the strength divine.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: And he fell where the pillars fell,
Eyes hollowed by love's deceit.
In the silence she sang him to sleep,
In the silence, he kissed defeat.

sections.label: Verse 3
lines: She wept behind the silver door,
Her palms still warm with broken trust.
She held the coin, but not his grace
Her name now buried in the dust.

sections.label: Verse 4
lines: Chains rusted where his glory stood,
Mocked beneath the Philistine sky.
But from the dark, he felt again
The winds of God begin to rise.
He stood between the ancient stones,
Blind, but clear in spirit's cry.
A prayer broke from his shattered lips,
"Let me die with them, let me die."

sections.label: Chorus
lines: – repeat)
And he fell where the pillars fell,
Eyes hollowed by love's deceit.
In the silence she sang him to sleep,
In the silence, he kissed defeat.

sections.label: Outro
lines: – atmospheric, layered vocals / soft fade)
Braids of fate now lost in dust,
A heart betrayed, a temple crushed.
But even ruin has its spark
He loved too much, he burned too hard.

epigraph: and they set him between the pillars
